type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: I'll own Image.py and implement both dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in save(). Please send SPEC for backup params: exact signature, defaults, behavior, interaction with existing save flow.

fields.note: I'll own Image.py and implement both dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in save(). Please send SPEC for backup params: exact signature, defaults, behavior, interaction with existing save flow.

content:
type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image.py
spec: Please add tests for the dry_run parameter on Image.save(). Requirements for the feature (I will implement in src/PIL/Image.py): save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params). When dry_run=True: the save handler still runs fully (format conversion/encoding happens, so errors like unsupported mode still raise), but no file is created/written on disk, and no existing file is truncated/modified. Returns None as usual. Suggested tests (add to Tests/test_image.py, e.g. near existing save tests): 1) test_dry_run_no_file_created: save to a new temp filename with dry_run=True, assert the file does NOT exist afterward. 2) test_dry_run_does_not_modify_existing_file: create a file with known bytes, call save(path, dry_run=True) on a different image, assert original file bytes are unchanged. 3) test_dry_run_raises_errors: e.g. saving a mode that is unsupported for a format (such as saving an 'RGBA' image as 'JPEG' without conversion, or another known error case) with dry_run=True still raises the same exception as a normal save. 4) test_dry_run_with_file_object: passing a BytesIO as fp with dry_run=True should not write bytes into it (getvalue() empty or unchanged). Use pytest idioms consistent with the rest of the file (helper_check_wrong_mode style, tmp_path fixture, etc.).
note: dry_run tests

fields.spec: Please add tests for the dry_run parameter on Image.save(). Requirements for the feature (I will implement in src/PIL/Image.py): save(fp, format=None, dry_run=False, **params). When dry_run=True: the save handler still runs fully (format conversion/encoding happens, so errors like unsupported mode still raise), but no file is created/written on disk, and no existing file is truncated/modified. Returns None as usual. Suggested tests (add to Tests/test_image.py, e.g. near existing save tests): 1) test_dry_run_no_file_created: save to a new temp filename with dry_run=True, assert the file does NOT exist afterward. 2) test_dry_run_does_not_modify_existing_file: create a file with known bytes, call save(path, dry_run=True) on a different image, assert original file bytes are unchanged. 3) test_dry_run_raises_errors: e.g. saving a mode that is unsupported for a format (such as saving an 'RGBA' image as 'JPEG' without conversion, or another known error case) with dry_run=True still raises the same exception as a normal save. 4) test_dry_run_with_file_object: passing a BytesIO as fp with dry_run=True should not write bytes into it (getvalue() empty or unchanged). Use pytest idioms consistent with the rest of the file (helper_check_wrong_mode style, tmp_path fixture, etc.).

files: src/PIL/Image.py
note: Implemented dry_run (mine) and backup/backup_suffix (yours) in Image.save(). Ran full Tests/test_image.py and all save-related tests across suite - all pass. Ready for your tests in test_image.py.
